logs.heading: 5 memories and 1 relevant solutions found

kvps.memories:
The application architecture (voicelog-scaffold) utilizes a standardized Work Order (WO) template inheritance hierarchy governed by a 'copy-on-inherit' model to ensure downstream isolation.

1. Scope & WO Hierarchy: System (scope='system', formerly 'app') -> Company (scope='company') -> Project (scope='project') -> Unit/Workspace (actual instance/DWO). Each level is editable and can reference SOP steps.

2. Implementation Details: DWOs are unit-level instances utilizing a 'not_required' toggle within the 'task_data' JSONB field to optimize data volume. Project-level templates are 'frozen' via PostgreSQL triggers when a project becomes 'active'.

3. Onboarding & Logic: The 'onboard.ts' API handles copying system-scope templates to company-scope during tenant onboarding, filtered by industry or trade. The 'app' scope has been fully renamed to 'system' across the database, API, and frontend.

4. Type Order (TO) Hierarchy: Defines workspaces at System, Company, and Project levels. TOs can be 'Atomic' or 'Composite'.

5. Database & Technical Status: Phase 1 schema evolution (Migration 20260304000001) introduced eight tables including trades, type_orders, and project_outcomes. Denormalized trade names are synced via triggers (sync_trade_name). As of March 2026, the API layer (api.ts) lacks CRUD methods for these new entities.

6. Documentation: System flexibility is detailed in '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/docs/general/system_flexibility.md'.

The 'voicelog-scaffold' (also referred to as 'voice-scaffold') project is a multi-tenant construction management and renovation platform for trades, focusing on timesheet and work-order management. It is organized as a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo and follows a 'schema-first' philosophy with Zod and a three-tier template inheritance system (App-Level, Company-Level, Project-Level).

Project Location:
- Container Path: /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold
- Local Path: 'C:\Users\Casey\voicelog-scaffold' (Windows) bind-mounted to the container path.

Template Logic:
- Inheritance: App -> Company -> Project.
- Freezing Mechanism: Project templates are 'frozen' (definitions copied from parents) when a project status changes to 'active' to ensure downstream isolation and prevent retroactive changes.

Architecture & Apps:
- API (:3000): 'The Brain'. Features 12 route modules and an A2A JSON-RPC protocol with a Placeholder Recovery system.
- Web Dashboard (:3001): Legacy interface.
- Material Admin (:3002): Manages supplier catalogs and inventory via JsonTreeEditor.
- Site Companion (:3003): Mobile PWA for field workers with voice-to-structured-data capture.
- Work Orders (:3004): Primary management for work order lifecycles and project documents.
- Tenant Portal (:3005): Custom-built web interface for organization-level configuration.
- doc-parser (:5000): FastAPI/Detectron2 for PDF analysis. Codebase located at /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/doc-parser/.

Environment & Tech Stack:
- Stack: SolidJS, TypeScript, Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, Hono, Supabase (PostgreSQL/Storage), NocoDB, Zod.
- LLM Integration: Uses `litellm` to communicate with providers (e.g., Anthropic/Claude).
- Shared Packages: @repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils.

Documentation is located in 'docs/general/platform_analysis.md' and 'docs/general/dbTables.md'.

Project: Tenant Portal for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Location: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/tenant-portal/` (Port 3005).

Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS.
- API: Hono (OpenAPIHono) on Node.js.
- Database: Supabase (^2.90.1) / PostgreSQL.

Core Requirements & Features:
- Architecture: Custom-built web interface integrated into the monorepo, designed to be embeddable via iframe.
- Routing: Hash-based using @solidjs/router (#/login, #/dashboard, #/table/:tableName, #/admin).
- Authentication: Supabase Auth supporting Email/Password and Magic Links.
- Data Management: Configuration-driven UI featuring dynamic table viewing and inline editing. Table visibility and editability are controlled per organization via the 'portal_table_config' table.
- Configuration Details: The 'portal_table_config' table manages per-organization/tenant access levels (hidden, readonly, editable) and column-level visibility (visible_columns, editable_columns, display preferences).
- Security: Strict tenant isolation using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies. The 'portal_table_config' table uses RLS and a `current_user_organization_id()` function to ensure organizations only access their own configuration.
- Administration: Admin section for organization-level configuration and managing table permissions.

SOP Library Manager & Work Order Integration Architecture:

1. Implementation Status: SOP ↔ Work Order Integration is fully implemented across API and Frontend layers as of 2026-03-10.

2. Database Schema (Supabase/pgvector): Includes 'sops' (master), 'sop_sections' (typed content), 'sop_steps' (unified checklists), 'sop_dependencies' (Mermaid graphs), 'sop_ref_docs' (shared trade references), and 'sop_embeddings' (vector search).

3. SOP-WO Bridge & Phase 1 Evolution: SOPs are linked to Work Orders (WOs) via `sop_id`. The integration utilizes 'work_order_step_progress' to track status, completion, and duration (specifically 'duration_minutes') per step. This enables step-level progress tracking and automated timesheet generation for the separate timesheet application. Other Phase 1 tables include 'bom_action_requests', 'trades', 'organization_trades', 'project_outcomes', 'trade_outcome_assignments', 'type_orders', 'type_order_compositions', and 'type_order_default_work_orders'.

4. Hybrid 'Docs-as-Code' Architecture: SOPs are managed via a 'docs-as-code' architecture using Git-based markdown files synced to a Supabase PostgreSQL layer. System components located at `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/skills/sop_library_manager` with a CLI/API in `scripts/sop_manager.py`.

5. Standards & Workflow: Supports trade-specific docs (PLB, ELC, etc.) with a 15-section structure. Follows a 7-step authoring workflow (Parse -> Index -> Plan -> REF -> Generate -> Update -> Report) using 'REF' docs for consistency.

6. Frontend: 'ProjectDetail' page supports matching/rejecting BOM requests from architectural documents and provides the interface for the SOP-WO integration.

The 'voicelog-scaffold' project is a multi-tenant construction management platform using a SolidJS frontend, Hono API, and Supabase/PostgreSQL database, organized as a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Design and implement a comprehensive workflow and logic for integrating Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) with a 3-tier Work Order (WO) template system (System -> Company -> Project -> Unit) and provide the full-stack implementation for tracking step progress.

# Solution
1. **Architecture & Documentation**: Created `SOP_WO_WORKFLOW.md` defining the architecture across SOP Library (Knowledge), Work Order System (Execution), and Timesheet App (Reporting). Uses a hierarchical inheritance model (System -> Company -> Project -> Unit) with a `freeze_project_templates()` PostgreSQL trigger for data integrity.
2. **API Layer (Hono/Supabase)**: Updated `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` to allow `sop_id` in PATCH requests. Implemented endpoints for SOP listing, detail retrieval, inherited SOP resolution, and work order step progress management (GET, POST for initialization, PATCH for updates).
3. **Data Ingestion & Storage**: Git/Markdown source of truth parsed into `sops`, `sop_sections`, and `sop_steps` tables. Uses MD5 hashing for change detection and 'Executive Checklist' extraction for trackable steps.
4. **Frontend API Client**: Updated `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` with `WorkOrderTemplate` updates and new interfaces (`SopSummary`, `SopSection`, `SopStep`, `StepProgress`). Added `ApiClient` methods for SOP and progress tracking.
5. **Execution & Progress Tracking**: 
   - **Bridge Table**: `work_order_step_progress` links `work_order_id` to `sop_step_id`.
   - **Lifecycle**: Creation -> Step Initialization -> Step Execution (tracking `status`, `duration_minutes`, `completed_by`).
6. **UI Implementation (SolidJS)**:
   - **Template Detail**: Added `SopLink` component in `TemplateDetail.tsx` using `createResource` to display linked SOP metadata.
   - **Work Order Detail**: Implemented `StepProgressPanel` in `WorkOrderDetail.tsx` for step initialization and status updates via dropdown.
7. **Workforce & Timesheet Integration**: Distinguishes between 'Employee' (duration-based) and 'Sub-contractor' (completion-based). API provides data for payroll and efficiency reporting (actual vs. estimated duration).

Work Order (WO) template inheritance follows a three-tier hierarchy: System -> Company -> Project (frozen when project activates).

kvps.memories:
Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are managed via a 'docs-as-code' architecture using Git-based markdown files synced to a Supabase PostgreSQL layer.

SOP Library Manager & Work Order Integration Architecture:

1. Implementation Status: SOP ↔ Work Order Integration is fully implemented across API and Frontend layers as of 2026-03-10. API endpoints for SOPs and step progress are specifically located in 'apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts'.

2. Database Schema (Supabase/pgvector): Core tables include 'sops' (master), 'sop_sections' (typed content), 'sop_steps' (unified checklists), 'sop_dependencies' (Mermaid graphs), 'sop_ref_docs' (shared trade references), and 'sop_embeddings' (vector search).

3. SOP-WO Bridge & Phase 1 Evolution: SOPs are linked to Work Orders (WOs) via `sop_id`. The integration utilizes 'work_order_step_progress' to track status, completion, and duration (specifically 'duration_minutes') per step. This enables step-level progress tracking and automated timesheet generation. Other Phase 1 tables include 'bom_action_requests', 'trades', 'organization_trades', 'project_outcomes', 'trade_outcome_assignments', 'type_orders', 'type_order_compositions', and 'type_order_default_work_orders'.

4. Hybrid 'Docs-as-Code' Architecture: SOPs are managed via a 'docs-as-code' architecture using Git-based markdown files synced to a Supabase PostgreSQL layer. System components located at `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/skills/sop_library_manager` with a CLI/API in `scripts/sop_manager.py`.

5. Standards & Workflow: Supports trade-specific docs (PLB, ELC, etc.) with a 15-section structure. Follows a 7-step authoring workflow (Parse -> Index -> Plan -> REF -> Generate -> Update -> Report) using 'REF' docs for consistency.

6. Frontend: 'ProjectDetail' page supports matching/rejecting BOM requests from architectural documents and provides the interface for the SOP-WO integration.

SOP Authoring Standard (SOP-META-001 Rev 1.3): Mandatory 15-section structure. Content must include Executive Checklists (limited to max 7 steps per phase) and Detailed Steps for technical execution.

The hybrid parsing pipeline (hybrid.py) integrates IBM Docling and PyMuPDF for architectural PDF extraction. A major update on 2026-03-06 resolved key regressions and refined detection logic: 1) Fixed inflated door/window counts by implementing page-level deduplication and updating URL storage to Dict[int, List[str]] to prevent data overwriting. 2) Refined detection thresholds in detection.py to prevent misclassifying architectural lines as tables. 3) Enhanced table extraction by supporting both Docling-extracted tables and LLM-based spec-table extraction for bounding-box-detected regions, saving results as markdown/JSON. The system utilizes a multi-layered deduplication strategy and CV2 grid analysis to ensure data integrity for finish schedules and architectural specifications.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Design and implement a comprehensive workflow for integrating Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) with a 3-tier Work Order (WO) template system (System -> Company -> Project -> Unit) and provide the full-stack implementation for tracking step progress in a Hono/Supabase/SolidJS stack.

# Solution
1. **Architecture & Documentation**: Created `SOP_WO_WORKFLOW.md` defining the architecture across SOP Library (Knowledge), Work Order System (Execution), and Timesheet App (Reporting). Uses a hierarchical inheritance model with a `freeze_project_templates()` PostgreSQL trigger for data integrity.
2. **API Layer (Hono/Supabase)**: In `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts`, added `sop_id` to `allowedFields`. Implemented endpoints: `GET /sops`, `GET /sops/:id`, `GET /templates/:id/sop` (inheritance resolution), `GET /work-orders/:id/step-progress`, `POST /work-orders/:id/initialize-steps`, and `PATCH /work-orders/step-progress/:id`.
3. **Data Ingestion & Storage**: Git/Markdown source of truth parsed into `sops`, `sop_sections`, and `sop_steps` tables. Uses MD5 hashing for change detection and 'Executive Checklist' extraction for trackable steps.
4. **Frontend API Client (SolidJS)**: In `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts`, updated `WorkOrderTemplate` interface and added `SopSummary`, `SopSection`, `SopStep`, and `StepProgress`. Implemented `ApiClient` methods for all new SOP and progress tracking endpoints.
5. **Execution & Progress Tracking**: 
   - **Bridge Table**: `work_order_step_progress` links `work_order_id` to `sop_step_id`.
   - **Lifecycle**: Creation -> Step Initialization -> Step Execution (tracking `status`, `duration_minutes`, `completed_by`).
6. **UI Implementation (SolidJS)**:
   - **Template Detail**: Added `SopLink` component in `TemplateDetail.tsx` using `createResource` to display SOP number, task name, and revision.
   - **Work Order Detail**: Implemented `StepProgressPanel` in `WorkOrderDetail.tsx` with an 'Initialize' button and status dropdowns (Pending, In Progress, Completed).
7. **Workforce & Timesheet Integration**: Distinguishes between 'Employee' (duration-based) and 'Sub-contractor' (completion-based) for payroll and efficiency reporting.

# Problem
Migrating, documenting, and restoring a complex live Supabase/PostgreSQL database schema (150+ tables) for local development. The goal is to filter internal metadata (NocoDB/system schemas), respect foreign key dependencies, and replace raw SQL dumps with structured, manageable migrations and documentation.

# Solution
1. **Extraction & Filtering**:
   - Use `pg_dump -s --no-owner` for schema-only extraction and `--data-only --inserts` for seed data.
   - Use Python/Bash scripts to filter out system schemas (`auth`, `storage`) and third-party prefixes (e.g., `nc_` or `xc_` from NocoDB).

2. **Structured Migration Generation**:
   - Parse the SQL dump and organize output into numbered files in `supabase/migrations/` following dependency order: Extensions -> Types -> Tables -> Constraints -> Functions -> Views -> RLS.
   - Example: `00001_extensions.sql`, `00002_tables.sql`.

3. **Dependency-Aware Seeding**:
   - Generate a `seed.sql` file by extracting data in an order that satisfies foreign key constraints.
   - **Circular Reference Handling**: Inject `SET session_replication_role = 'replica';` at the start of the seed file and reset to `'origin'` at the end to bypass trigger and constraint checks during bulk insertion.

4. **Documentation & Verification**:
   - **Documentation**: Rewrite raw SQL into Markdown organized by domain (Core, AI, etc.). Include RLS status (🔒 badge), JSONB structure examples, and an ASCII 'Entity Relationship Overview'.
   - **Deployment**: Execute `supabase db reset` to apply clean migrations and verify row counts/schema integrity against the source.
   - Document PostgreSQL views with aggregation logic descriptions.

# SOP Library Manager

## Metadata
- **Name**: sop_library_manager
- **Version**: 1.1.0
- **Description**: Manages the SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) library in Supabase Storage and Database. Handles uploading, parsing, syncing, searching, and agent-driven creation of SOPs.
- **Tags**: sop, library, supabase, storage, database, procedures, trade, plumbing, electrical, hvac
- **Author**: Agent Zero
- **Created**: 2026-02-28
- **Updated**: 2026-02-28

## Overview

This skill provides a complete lifecycle manager for Standard Operating Procedures stored as `.md` files in Supabase Storage (`apps/voicelog/sops/`). It parses structured SOP markdown into database tables for fast retrieval of executive checklists, detailed steps, and recommended videos.

### Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   SOP Library Flow                       │
│                                                         │
│  .md File ──► Upload to Storage ──► Parse Markdown      │
│                                         │               │
│                                         ▼               │
│                              ┌──────────────────┐       │
│                              │   sops (master)   │       │
│                              └────────┬─────────┘       │
│                    ┌──────────────────┼──────────────┐   │
│                    ▼                  ▼              ▼   │
│          sop_executive_    sop_detailed_   sop_recommended│
│          checklists        steps           _videos        │
│                                                         │
│  Search/Query ◄── Database Tables (fast retrieval)      │
│  Missing SOP? ──► Agent Request ──► Create + Upload     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Database Tables

| Table | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `sops` | Master index — SOP number, trade, task, revision, status, storage path, file hash |
| `sop_executive_checklists` | Cached executive checklist items per procedure phase |
| `sop_detailed_steps` | Cached detailed step content per procedure phase |
| `sop_recommended_videos` | Cached video links organized by procedure phase |
| `sop_sync_log` | Audit trail for all library operations |
| `v_sop_library` | Convenience view with content counts |

### Storage

- **Bucket**: `apps`
- **Path**: `voicelog/sops/{filename}.md`
- **Format**: Structured markdown following SOP Authoring Guide (SOP-META-001)

## Prerequisites

- Supabase instance running (local or remote)
- Migration `20260228000001_sop_library.sql` applied
- Python packages: `supabase` (auto-installed if missing)

## Procedures

### 1. Upload an SOP to the Library

Uploads a `.md` file to Supabase Storage, parses it, and syncs all structured data to the database.

```bash
cd /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py upload <path-to-sop.md>
```

**Example:**
```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py upload docs/sop/SOP_Plumbing_Sink_Replacement.md
```

**Output:** JSON with sop_id, sop_number, sync counts for checklists/steps/videos.

### 2. Sync Database from Storage

Re-parses SOP files from storage and updates the database. Detects changes via MD5 hash.

```bash
# Sync all SOPs
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py sync --all

# Sync a specific SOP
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py sync PLB-SOP-001
```

### 3. Search the Library

Search by keyword across SOP number, trade, task name, and description.

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py search "plumbing"
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py search "outlet"
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py search "sink"
```

### 4. List All SOPs

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py list
```

### 5. Show SOP Details with Cached Data

Retrieves the full SOP record plus all cached checklists, steps, and videos from the database.

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py show PLB-SOP-001
```

### 6. Check if SOP Exists — Request Creation if Not

This is the key workf

──►│ sop_recommended_   │  │  │
│                  │                │ videos             │  │  │

│                  │        ┌────────────────────┐          │  │

│                  │        │  (audit trail)     │          │  │

│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Data Flow

1. **Author** writes an SOP in markdown following the [SOP Authoring Guide](../../docs/sop/SOP_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md)
2. **Upload** pushes the file to Supabase Storage bucket `apps/voicelog/sops/`
3. **Parser** extracts structured data using regex patterns:
   - Metadata (SOP number, trade, revision, etc.)
   - Executive checklists (phase → step → task mapping)
   - Detailed steps (section → step → content)
   - Recommended videos (title, URL, phase mapping)
4. **Database** stores parsed data in normalized tables for fast retrieval
5. **Agents/Apps** query via CLI or Python API

---

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Supabase running locally (or remotely with URL configured)
- Migration `20260228000001_sop_library.sql` applied
- Python 3.10+ with `supabase` package installed

### 1. Install Dependencies

```bash
pip install supabase
```

### 2. Upload Your First SOP

```bash
cd /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py upload docs/sop/SOP_Plumbing_Sink_Replacement.md
```

### 3. Verify It Worked

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py list
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py show PLB-SOP-001
```

### 4. Search the Library

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py search "plumbing"
```

---

## CLI Reference

All commands are run from the `voicelog-scaffold/` directory:

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py <command> [args]
```

### `upload <file>`

Upload an SOP markdown file to Supabase Storage and sync to database.

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py upload docs/sop/SOP_Plumbing_Sink_Replacement.md
```

**Output:** JSON with `sop_id`, `sop_number`, and sync counts.

---

### `sync [--all | <sop_number>]`

Re-parse and sync database from storage. Detects changes via MD5 hash.

```bash
# Sync all SOPs
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py sync --all

# Sync specific SOP
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py sync PLB-SOP-001
```

---

### `search <query>`

Keyword search across SOP number, trade name, task name, and description.

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py search "electrical"
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py search "sink"
```

**Output:** JSON array of matching SOP records.

---

### `list`

List all SOPs in the library with key metadata.

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py list
```

**Output:** JSON array with `sop_number`, `trade_code`, `trade_name`, `task_name`, `revision`, `status`, `storage_path`.

---

### `show <sop_number>`

Retrieve full SOP details including all cached checklists, steps, and videos.

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py show PLB-SOP-001
```

**Output:** JSON object with keys: `sop`, `executive_checklists`, `detailed_steps`, `recommended_videos`.

---

### `check <trade> <task>`

Check if a matching SOP exists. If not, generate an agent creation request.

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py check "Plumbing" "Water Heater Replacement"
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py check "HVAC" "Thermostat Installation"
```

**If found:** `{ "found": true, "matches": [...] }`
**If not found:** `{ "found": false, "agent_request": { "action": "create_sop", "prompt": "..." } }`

---

### `request <trade> <task>`

Force-generate an agent creation request (even if SOP exists).

```bash
python skills/sop_library_manager/scripts/sop_manager.py request "Carpentry" "Door Frame Installation"
```

---

## Python API Reference

```python
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, 'skills/sop_library_manager/scripts')
from sop_manager import SOPLibraryManager

mgr = SOPLibraryManager()
```

### `mgr.upload(file_path: str) -> dict`

Upload a local `.md` file to storage and sync to database.

```python
result = mgr.upload('docs/sop/SOP_Plumbing_Sink_Replacement.md')
# Returns: { "status": "synced", "sop_id": "...", "sop_number": "PLB-SOP-001", ... }
```

### `mgr.search(query: str) -> list`

Search SOPs by keyword.

```python
results = mgr.search('plumbing')
for sop in results:
    print(sop['sop_number'], sop['task_name'])
```

### `mgr.list_all() -> list`

List all SOPs with summary metadata.

```python
for sop in mgr.list_all():
    print(sop['sop_number'], sop['trade_name'], sop['status'])
```

### `mgr.show(sop_number: str) -> dict`

Get full SOP details with all cached data.

```python
details = mgr.show('PLB-SOP-001')
print(details['sop']['task_name'])
print(f"Checklists: {len(details['executive_checklists'])}")
print(f"Steps: {len(details['detailed_steps'])}")
print(f"Videos: {len(details['recommended_videos'])}")
```

### `mgr.check_and_request(trade: str, task: str) -> dict`

Check if SOP exists; generate creation request if not.

```python
result = mgr.check_and_request('HVAC', 'Thermostat Installation')
if result['found']:
    print(f"Found {len(result['matches'])} match(es)")
else:
    print(f"Request: {result['agent_request']['prompt']}")
```

### `mgr.sync_file(storage_path: str) -> dict`

Sync a specific file from storage to database.

```python
result = mgr.sync_file('voicelog/sops/SOP_Plumbing_Sink_Replacement.md')
```

### `mgr.sync_all() -> list`

Sync all SOP files from storage to database.

```python
results = mgr.sync_all()
for r in results:
    print(r['sop_number'], r['status'])
```

---

## Database Schema

### `sops` (Master Table)

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | uuid | Primary key |
| `sop_number` | text | Unique identifier (e.g., PLB-SOP-001) |
| `trade_code` | text | 3-letter trade code (PLB, ELC, HVA) |
| `trade_name` | text | Full trade name |
| `task_name` | text | Task description |
| `revision` | text | Version string |
| `effective_date` | text | Date SOP became effective |
| `description` | text | Brief description |
| `scope` | text | Scope of the procedure |
| `status` | text | active, draft, archived |
| `storage_path` | text | Path in Supabase Storage |
| `file_hash` | text | MD5 hash for change detection |
| `tags` | text[] | Searchable tags |

### `sop_executive_checklists`

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sop_id` | uuid | FK → sops.id |
| `section_type` | text | "removal" or "installation" |
| `section_number` | int | Section number in document |
| `step_number` | int | Step within section |
| `step_name` | text | Phase/group name |
| `tasks` | text | Comma-separated task descriptions |
| `is_checked` | bool | Completion status |

### `sop_detailed_steps`

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sop_id` | uuid | FK → sops.id |
| `section_type` | text | "removal" or "installation" |
| `section_number` | int | Section number |
| `step_number` | int | Step number |
| `step_title` | text | Step heading |
| `content` | text | Full step content (markdown) |

### `sop_recommended_videos`

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sop_id` | uuid | FK → sops.id |
| `title` | text | Video title |
| `url` | text | YouTube/video URL |
| `procedure_phase` | text | Which phase the video covers |
| `sort_order` | int | Display order |

### `sop_sync_log`

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sop_id` | uuid | FK → sops.id (nullable) |
| `sop_number` | text | SOP number |
| `action` | text | uploaded, synced, agent_requested, etc. |
| `details` | jsonb | Action-specific metadata |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | When the action occurred |

---

## File Structure

```
skills/sop_library_manager/
├── README.md                 # This file — human-readable overview
├── AGENT_GUIDE.md            # Step-by-step guide for Agent Zero agents
├── SKILL.md                  # Skill manifest (agentskills.io standard)
└── scripts/
    └── sop_manager.py        # Main CLI + Python API (single file)
```

### Related Project Files

| File | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| DB Migration | `supabase/migrations/20260228000001_sop_library.sql` | Creates all tables |
| Authoring Guide | `docs/sop/SOP_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md` | How to write SOPs |
| SOP Template | `docs/sop/SOP_TEMPLATE.md` | Blank SOP starter |
| Library Index | `docs/sop/SOP_INDEX.md` | Master registry |
| Plumbing SOP | `docs/sop/SOP_Plumbing_Sink_Replacement.md` | Example |
| Electrical SOP | `docs/sop/SOP_Electrical_Outlet_Replacement.md` | Example |

---

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SUPABASE_URL` | `http://host.docker.internal:54321` | Supabase API endpoint |
| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | Local dev key | Service role key for admin access |

> **Note**: When running inside Docker, use `host.docker.internal` to reach the host's Supabase. When running directly on the host, use `127.0.0.1`.

---

## Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `Connection refused` | Supabase not running | Run `supabase start` on host |
| `relation "sops" does not exist` | Migration not applied | Run `supabase db reset` or apply migration SQL |
| `Bucket not found` | First run | Script auto-creates bucket; check storage permissions |
| `Parse returns 0 items` | SOP format mismatch | Verify SOP follows Authoring Guide exactly |
| `Hash unchanged, skipping` | File identical to last sync | Content hasn't changed; force with `sync` command |
| `Connection refused` (Docker) | Wrong URL | Set `SUPABASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:54321` |

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""SOP Library Manager — Supabase Storage + Database Sync

Manages the SOP library lifecycle:
  - Upload .md files to Supabase Storage (apps/voicelog/sops/)
  - Parse SOP markdown into structured data
  - Sync parsed data to database tables (sops, sop_executive_checklists, sop_detailed_steps, sop_recommended_videos)
  - Detect changes via MD5 hashing
  - Search/query the library
  - Request agent creation of missing SOPs

Usage:
  python sop_manager.py upload   <file.md>              Upload SOP to storage + parse + sync DB
  python sop_manager.py sync     [--all | <sop_number>]  Re-parse and sync DB from storage
  python sop_manager.py search   <query>                 Search SOPs by keyword/trade/tag
  python sop_manager.py list                              List all SOPs in the library
  python sop_manager.py show     <sop_number>            Show SOP details with cached data
  python sop_manager.py request  <trade> <task>           Request agent to create a new SOP
  python sop_manager.py check    <trade> <task>           Check if suitable SOP exists, request if not
"""

import sys
import os
import re
import json
import hashlib
import argparse
from datetime import datetime, timezone

try:
    from supabase import create_client, Client
except ImportError:
    print("Installing supabase-py...")
    os.system("pip install supabase")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SUPABASE_URL = os.environ.get("SUPABASE_URL", "http://host.docker.internal:54321")
SUPABASE_KEY = os.environ.get("SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY",
    "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZS1kZW1vIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImV4cCI6MTk4MzgxMjk5Nn0.EGIM96RAZx35lJzdJsyH-qQwv8Hdp7fsn3W0YpN81IU")
STORAGE_BUCKET = "apps"
STORAGE_PREFIX = "voicelog/sops"


def get_client() -> Client:
    return create_client(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_KEY)


def md5_hash(content: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.md5(content.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SOP Markdown Parser
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class SOPParser:
    """Parses a structured SOP .md file into components."""

    def __init__(self, content: str):
        self.content = content
        self.lines = content.split("\n")

    def parse_header_table(self) -> dict:
        """Extract the header metadata table (Field | Detail format)."""
        meta = {}
        in_table = False
        for line in self.lines:
            # Detect table start: header row with Field/Detail or SOP Number
            if "|" in line and ("Field" in line and "Detail" in line) and "---" not in line:
                in_table = True
                continue
            # Skip separator row
            if in_table and "|" in line and re.match(r"^[\|\s\-:]+$", line):
                continue
            if in_table and "|" in line:
                cells = [c.strip() for c in line.split("|") if c.strip()]
                if len(cells) >= 2:
                    # Strip bold markdown markers
                    key = re.sub(r"\*\*", "", cells[0]).strip()
                    val = re.sub(r"\*\*", "", cells[1]).strip()
                    if key:
                        meta[key] = val
            elif in_table and "|" not in line and line.strip() and line.strip() != "---":
                break
        return meta

    def parse_sections(self) -> list:
        """Split document into sections by ## headings."""
        sections = []
        current = None
        for line in self.lines:
            m = re.match(r"^## (\d+)\.\s+(.+)$", line)
            if m:
                if current:
                    sections.append(current)
                current = {
                    "number": int(m.group(1)),
                    "title": m.group(2).strip(),
                    "content": ""
                }
            elif current:
                current["content"] += line + "\n"
        if current:
            sections.append(current)
        return sections

    def parse_executive_checklist(self, section_cont

            self.client.table("sops").update(sop_data).eq("id", sop_id).execute()
        else:
            result = self.client.table("sops").insert(sop_data).execute()
            sop_id = result.data[0]["id"]

        # Clear old cached data
        self.client.table("sop_executive_checklists").delete().eq("sop_id", sop_id).execute()
        self.client.table("sop_detailed_steps").delete().eq("sop_id", sop_id).execute()

        # Insert checklists
        for cl in parsed["checklists"]:
            cl["sop_id"] = sop_id

        for ds in parsed["detailed_steps"]:
            ds["sop_id"] = sop_id
            self.client.table("sop_detailed_steps").insert(ds).execute()

        for vid in parsed["videos"]:
            vid["sop_id"] = sop_id
            self.client.table("sop_recommended_videos").insert(vid).execute()

        return {
            "sop_id": sop_id,
            "sop_number": sop_number,
            "status": "synced",
            "checklists": len(parsed["checklists"]),
            "detailed_steps": len(parsed["detailed_steps"]),
            "videos": len(parsed["videos"]),
            "message": f"{sop_number} synced: {len(parsed['checklists'])} checklists, {len(parsed['detailed_steps'])} steps, {len(parsed['videos'])} videos"
        }

    def sync_all(self) -> list:
        """Re-sync all SOPs from storage."""
        results = []
        sops = self.client.table("sops").select("sop_number, storage_path").execute()
        for sop in sops.data:
            try:
                data = self.client.storage.from_(STORAGE_BUCKET).download(sop["storage_path"])
                content = data.decode("utf-8")
                file_hash = md5_hash(content)
                result = self._sync_from_content(content, sop["storage_path"], file_hash)
                results.append(result)
            except Exception as e:
                results.append({"sop_number": sop["sop_number"], "status": "error", "message": str(e)})
        return results

    def sync_one(self, sop_number: str) -> dict:
        """Re-sync a single SOP from storage."""
        sop = self.client.table("sops").select("storage_path").eq("sop_number", sop_number).execute()
        if not sop.data:
            return {"status": "error", "message": f"SOP {sop_number} not found in database"}
        try:
            data = self.client.storage.from_(STORAGE_BUCKET).download(sop.data[0]["storage_path"])
            content = data.decode("utf-8")
            file_hash = md5_hash(content)
            return self._sync_from_content(content, sop.data[0]["storage_path"], file_hash)
        except Exception as e:
            return {"status": "error", "message": str(e)}

    def search(self, query: str) -> list:
        """Search SOPs by keyword in sop_number, trade, task, description, tags."""
        q = query.lower()
        results = self.client.table("sops").select("*").or_(
            f"sop_number.ilike.%{q}%,trade_name.ilike.%{q}%,task_name.ilike.%{q}%,description.ilike.%{q}%"
        ).execute()
        return results.data

    def list_all(self) -> list:
        """List all SOPs."""
        results = self.client.table("sops").select(
            "sop_number, trade_code, trade_name, task_name, revision, status, storage_path"
        ).order("sop_number").execute()
        return results.data

    def show(self, sop_number: str) -> dict:
        """Show full SOP details with cached data."""
        sop = self.client.table("sops").select("*").eq("sop_number", sop_number).execute()
        if not sop.data:
            return {"error": f"SOP {sop_number} not found"}
        sop_id = sop.data[0]["id"]
        checklists = self.client.table("sop_executive_checklists").select("*").eq("sop_id", sop_id).order("section_number,step_number").execute()
        steps = self.client.table("sop_detailed_steps").select("*").eq("sop_id", sop_id).order("section_number,step_number").execute()
        videos = self.client.table("sop_recommended_videos").select("*").eq("sop_id", sop_id).order("sort_order").execute()
        return {
            "sop": sop.data[0],
            "executive_checklists": checklists.data,
            "detailed_steps": steps.data,
            "recommended_videos": videos.data
        }

    def check_and_request(self, trade: str, task: str) -> dict:
        """Check if a suitable SOP exists. If not, return a request payload for agent creation."""
        results = self.search(f"{trade} {task}")
        if results:
            return {
                "found": True,
                "matches": results,
                "message": f"Found {len(results)} matching SOP(s)"
            }
        # No match — generate agent request
        trade_code = trade[:3].upper()
        # Find next number
        existing = self.client.table("sops").select("sop_number").ilike("sop_number", f"{trade_code}%").execute()
        max_num = 0
        for s in existing.data:
            m = re.search(r"(\d+)$", s["sop_number"])
            if m:
                max_num = max(max_num, int(m.group(1)))
        next_number = f"{trade_code}-SOP-{max_num + 1:03d}"

        request_payload = {
            "found": False,
            "message": f"No SOP found for '{trade} — {task}'. Agent creation requested.",
            "agent_request": {
                "action": "create_sop",
                "sop_number": next_number,
                "trade_code": trade_code,
                "trade_name": trade,
                "task_name": task,
                "prompt": (
                    f"Create a complete Standard Operating Procedure for {trade} — {task}. "
                    f"Assign SOP number {next_number}. Follow the SOP Authoring Guide (SOP-META-001) exactly. "
                    f"Include all mandatory sections: Purpose, Scope, References & Codes, Personnel, Safety, "
                    f"Tools & Equipment, Pre-Work Assessment, Procedure phases with Executive Checklists and "
                    f"Detailed Steps, Recommended Videos, Testing & Commissioning, Cleanup, Troubleshooting, "
                    f"Quality Standards, and Revision History. "
                    f"Save the file as {next_number.replace('-', '_')}_{task.replace(' ', '_')}.md"
                )
            }
        }

        # Log the request
        self._log_action(None, next_number, "agent_requested",
                         {"trade": trade, "task": task})
        return request_payload

    def _log_action(self, sop_id, sop_number, action, details=None):
        """Log a sync action."""
        try:
            self.client.table("sop_sync_log").insert({
                "sop_id": sop_id,
                "sop_number": sop_number,
                "action": action,
                "details": details or {}
            }).execute()
        except Exception:
            pass  # non-critical


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLI
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="SOP Library Manager")
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command")

    # upload
    p_upload = sub.add_parser("upload", help="Upload SOP .md file to storage and sync DB")
    p_upload.add_argument("file", help="Path to .md file")

    # sync
    p_sync = sub.add_parser("sync", help="Re-sync DB from storage")
    p_sync.add_argument("--all", action="store_true", help="Sync all SOPs")
    p_sync.add_argument("sop_number", nargs="?", help="Specific SOP number to sync")

    # search
    p_search = sub.add_parser("search", help="Search SOPs")
    p_search.add_argument("query", help="Search query")

    # list
    sub.add_parser("list", help="List all SOPs")

    # show
    p_show = sub.add_parser("show", help="Show SOP details")
    p_show.add_argument("sop_number", help="SOP number")

    # request
    p_req = sub.add_parser("request", help="Request agent to create SOP")
    p_req.add_argument("trade", help="Trade name")
    p_req.add_argument("task", help="Task name")

    # check
    p_check = sub.add_parser("check", help="Check if SOP exists, request if not")
    p_check.add_argument("trade", help="Trade name")
    p_check.add_argument("task", help="Task name")

    args = parser.parse_args()
    mgr = SOPLibraryManager()

    if args.command == "upload":
        result = mgr.upload_sop(args.file)
        print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

    elif args.command == "sync":
        if args.all:
            results = mgr.sync_all()
            for r in results:
                print(json.dumps(r, indent=2, default=str))
        elif args.sop_number:
            result = mgr.sync_one(args.sop_number)
            print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))
        else:
            print("Specify --all or a specific SOP number")

    elif args.command == "search":
        results = mgr.search(args.query)
        for r in results:
            print(f"  {r['sop_number']:15s} {r['trade_name']:15s} {r['task_name']:30s} [{r['status']}]")
        if not results:
            print("  No SOPs found.")

    elif args.command == "list":
        results = mgr.list_all()
        print(f"{'SOP Number':15s} {'Trade':10s} {'Task':30s} {'Rev':5s} {'Status':10s}")
        print("-" * 75)
        for r in results:
            print(f"{r['sop_number']:15s} {r['trade_code']:10s} {r['task_name']:30s} {r['revision']:5s} {r['status']:10s}")
        if not results:
            print("  Library is empty.")

    elif args.command == "show":
        result = mgr.show(args.sop_number)
        print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

    elif args.command == "request":
        result = mgr.check_and_request(args.trade, args.task)
        print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

    elif args.command == "check":
        result = mgr.check_and_request(args.trade, args.task)
        print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

    else:
        parser.print_help()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

=== SOP WO Bridge Migration ===
-- =============================================================================
-- Migration: SOP ↔ Work Order Bridge
-- Description:
--   1A. sop_sections — unified hierarchical SOP structure (replaces flat checklists/steps)
--   1B. sop_steps — individual trackable steps (one row = one checklist item = one RAG chunk)
--   1C. sop_dependencies — directed graph of SOP relationships
--   1D. work_order_step_progress — step-level completion tracking for unit WOs
--   1E. sop_ref_docs — trade-level reference documents
--   1F. Add ref_doc_id to sops
--   1G. Add sop_step_id to sop_recommended_videos
--   1H. Update freeze_project_templates() to preserve sop_id
--   1I. RLS policies for all new tables
--   1J. Updated_at triggers for all new tables
-- Date: 2026-03-10
-- =============================================================================

-- =============================================================================
-- 1A. sop_sections — structured sections parsed from SOP markdown files
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.sop_sections (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    sop_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sops(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    section_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (section_type IN (
        'purpose', 'scope', 'references', 'personnel', 'safety',
        'tools', 'pre_work', 'procedure', 'videos', 'testing',
        'cleanup', 'troubleshooting', 'quality', 'revision_history'
    )),
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    content_md TEXT,
    content_plain TEXT,
    is_ref_pointer BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    ref_section TEXT,
    phase_name TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE(sop_id, section_number)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.sop_sections IS 'Structured sections parsed from SOP markdown files';
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_sections_sop ON public.sop_sections(sop_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_sections_type ON public.sop_sections(section_type);

-- =============================================================================
-- 1B. sop_steps — individual trackable steps
--     Each row = one trackable unit of work = one RAG chunk
--     This is the table that work_order_step_progress references
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.sop_steps (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    sop_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sops(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sop_sections(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    phase_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    phase_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    step_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    step_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    tasks_summary TEXT,
    detail_md TEXT,
    detail_plain TEXT,
    est_duration_minutes INTEGER,
    requires_photo BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    requires_measurement BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE(sop_id, phase_number, step_number)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.sop_steps IS 'Individual trackable steps parsed from SOP procedure phases. One row = one checklist item = one RAG chunk.';
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_steps_sop ON public.sop_steps(sop_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_steps_section ON public.sop_steps(section_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_steps_phase ON public.sop_steps(sop_id, phase_number);

-- =============================================================================
-- 1C. sop_dependencies — directed graph of SOP relationships
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.sop_dependencies (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    from_sop_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sops(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    to_sop_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sops(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    link_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (link_type IN (
        'prerequisite', 'sub_procedure', 'cross_reference', 'optional'
    )),
    condition_note TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE(from_sop_id, to_sop_id, link_type)
);

);

-- RLS for trades
ALTER TABLE public.trades ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "trades_select_system" ON public.trades FOR SELECT
    USING (scope = 'system');

kvps.memories:
Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are managed via a 'docs-as-code' architecture using Git-based markdown files synced to a Supabase PostgreSQL layer.

SOP Library Manager & Work Order Integration Architecture:

1. Implementation Status: SOP ↔ Work Order Integration is fully implemented across API and Frontend layers as of 2026-03-10. API endpoints for SOPs and step progress are specifically located in 'apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts'.

2. Database Schema (Supabase/pgvector): Core tables include 'sops' (master), 'sop_sections' (typed content), 'sop_steps' (unified checklists), 'sop_dependencies' (Mermaid graphs), 'sop_ref_docs' (shared trade references), and 'sop_embeddings' (vector search).

3. SOP-WO Bridge & Phase 1 Evolution: SOPs are linked to Work Orders (WOs) via `sop_id`. The integration utilizes 'work_order_step_progress' to track status, completion, and duration (specifically 'duration_minutes') per step. This enables step-level progress tracking and automated timesheet generation. Other Phase 1 tables include 'bom_action_requests', 'trades', 'organization_trades', 'project_outcomes', 'trade_outcome_assignments', 'type_orders', 'type_order_compositions', and 'type_order_default_work_orders'.

4. Hybrid 'Docs-as-Code' Architecture: SOPs are managed via a 'docs-as-code' architecture using Git-based markdown files synced to a Supabase PostgreSQL layer. System components located at `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/skills/sop_library_manager` with a CLI/API in `scripts/sop_manager.py`.

5. Standards & Workflow: Supports trade-specific docs (PLB, ELC, etc.) with a 15-section structure. Follows a 7-step authoring workflow (Parse -> Index -> Plan -> REF -> Generate -> Update -> Report) using 'REF' docs for consistency.

6. Frontend: 'ProjectDetail' page supports matching/rejecting BOM requests from architectural documents and provides the interface for the SOP-WO integration.

SOP Authoring Standard (SOP-META-001 Rev 1.3): Mandatory 15-section structure. Content must include Executive Checklists (limited to max 7 steps per phase) and Detailed Steps for technical execution.

The hybrid parsing pipeline (hybrid.py) integrates IBM Docling and PyMuPDF for architectural PDF extraction. A major update on 2026-03-06 resolved key regressions and refined detection logic: 1) Fixed inflated door/window counts by implementing page-level deduplication and updating URL storage to Dict[int, List[str]] to prevent data overwriting. 2) Refined detection thresholds in detection.py to prevent misclassifying architectural lines as tables. 3) Enhanced table extraction by supporting both Docling-extracted tables and LLM-based spec-table extraction for bounding-box-detected regions, saving results as markdown/JSON. The system utilizes a multi-layered deduplication strategy and CV2 grid analysis to ensure data integrity for finish schedules and architectural specifications.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Design and implement a comprehensive workflow for integrating Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) with a 3-tier Work Order (WO) template system (System -> Company -> Project -> Unit) and provide the full-stack implementation for tracking step progress in a Hono/Supabase/SolidJS stack.

# Solution
1. **Architecture & Documentation**: Created `SOP_WO_WORKFLOW.md` defining the architecture across SOP Library (Knowledge), Work Order System (Execution), and Timesheet App (Reporting). Uses a hierarchical inheritance model with a `freeze_project_templates()` PostgreSQL trigger for data integrity.
2. **API Layer (Hono/Supabase)**: In `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts`, added `sop_id` to `allowedFields`. Implemented endpoints: `GET /sops`, `GET /sops/:id`, `GET /templates/:id/sop` (inheritance resolution), `GET /work-orders/:id/step-progress`, `POST /work-orders/:id/initialize-steps`, and `PATCH /work-orders/step-progress/:id`.
3. **Data Ingestion & Storage**: Git/Markdown source of truth parsed into `sops`, `sop_sections`, and `sop_steps` tables. Uses MD5 hashing for change detection and 'Executive Checklist' extraction for trackable steps.
4. **Frontend API Client (SolidJS)**: In `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts`, updated `WorkOrderTemplate` interface and added `SopSummary`, `SopSection`, `SopStep`, and `StepProgress`. Implemented `ApiClient` methods for all new SOP and progress tracking endpoints.
5. **Execution & Progress Tracking**: 
   - **Bridge Table**: `work_order_step_progress` links `work_order_id` to `sop_step_id`.
   - **Lifecycle**: Creation -> Step Initialization -> Step Execution (tracking `status`, `duration_minutes`, `completed_by`).
6. **UI Implementation (SolidJS)**:
   - **Template Detail**: Added `SopLink` component in `TemplateDetail.tsx` using `createResource` to display SOP number, task name, and revision.
   - **Work Order Detail**: Implemented `StepProgressPanel` in `WorkOrderDetail.tsx` with an 'Initialize' button and status dropdowns (Pending, In Progress, Completed).
7. **Workforce & Timesheet Integration**: Distinguishes between 'Employee' (duration-based) and 'Sub-contractor' (completion-based) for payroll and efficiency reporting.

# Problem
Migrating, documenting, and restoring a complex live Supabase/PostgreSQL database schema (150+ tables) for local development. The goal is to filter internal metadata (NocoDB/system schemas), respect foreign key dependencies, and replace raw SQL dumps with structured, manageable migrations and documentation.

# Solution
1. **Extraction & Filtering**:
   - Use `pg_dump -s --no-owner` for schema-only extraction and `--data-only --inserts` for seed data.
   - Use Python/Bash scripts to filter out system schemas (`auth`, `storage`) and third-party prefixes (e.g., `nc_` or `xc_` from NocoDB).

2. **Structured Migration Generation**:
   - Parse the SQL dump and organize output into numbered files in `supabase/migrations/` following dependency order: Extensions -> Types -> Tables -> Constraints -> Functions -> Views -> RLS.
   - Example: `00001_extensions.sql`, `00002_tables.sql`.

3. **Dependency-Aware Seeding**:
   - Generate a `seed.sql` file by extracting data in an order that satisfies foreign key constraints.
   - **Circular Reference Handling**: Inject `SET session_replication_role = 'replica';` at the start of the seed file and reset to `'origin'` at the end to bypass trigger and constraint checks during bulk insertion.

4. **Documentation & Verification**:
   - **Documentation**: Rewrite raw SQL into Markdown organized by domain (Core, AI, etc.). Include RLS status (🔒 badge), JSONB structure examples, and an ASCII 'Entity Relationship Overview'.
   - **Deployment**: Execute `supabase db reset` to apply clean migrations and verify row counts/schema integrity against the source.
   - Document PostgreSQL views with aggregation logic descriptions.

=== PNT Interior Wall Painting SOP ===
# Standard Operating Procedure
## Painting — Interior Wall Painting

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **SOP Number** | PNT-SOP-001 |
| **Trade** | Painting |
| **Task** | Interior wall painting — primer and 2-coat finish system |
| **Revision** | 1.0 |
| **Effective Date** | 2026-03-08 |
| **Approved By** | DRAFT |
| **Estimated Duration** | 4–8 hrs per room (varies by size and condition) |

---

## 1. Purpose

This SOP establishes the standard procedure for preparing, priming, and painting interior wall surfaces with a 2-coat latex finish system. It ensures consistent surface preparation, coverage, and final appearance across all residential and light commercial interior painting jobs.

---

## 2. Scope

This SOP covers:
- Interior wall surfaces: drywall, plaster, and previously painted surfaces
- Minor surface repairs (holes ≤ 3" diameter, hairline cracks)
- Application of primer coat on new, repaired, or stained surfaces
- Application of 2-coat interior latex finish

This SOP does **not** cover:
- Ceiling painting — treated as a separate scope; consult Operations Manager for procedure
- Trim and baseboard painting (see PNT-SOP-004: Trim & Baseboard Painting)
- Cabinet repainting (see PNT-SOP-003: Cabinet Repainting)
- Exterior wall or siding painting (see PNT-SOP-002: Exterior Siding Painting)
- Drywall repairs larger than 3" diameter (see DRW-SOP-001: Drywall Patch Repair)
- Active moisture, mold, or water-damage remediation (see DRW-SOP-004: Water Damage Repair)

---

## 3. References & Codes

See [REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md](REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md) — Painting Trade: Codes & Standards Reference.

---

## 4. Personnel & Qualifications

See [REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md](REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 4: Personnel & Qualifications.

---

## 5. Safety Requirements

### 5.1 Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)

See [REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md](REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 5: Standard PPE.

### 5.2 Hazard Awareness

See [REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md](REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 6: Common Hazard Awareness.

**Additional hazards for this SOP:**

| Hazard | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Slip hazard from paint spills | Fully cover floors with drop cloths; clean drips immediately; secure cloth edges with tape |
| Fire / flammability (solvent products) | No open flame near solvent-based paints; ensure ventilation; keep ABC extinguisher accessible |

### 5.3 Pre-Work Safety Checklist

- [ ] Ventilation confirmed: windows open, fans positioned to exhaust fumes to exterior
- [ ] Lead paint test completed on any surface to be sanded in pre-1978 structure
- [ ] Ladder inspected — all locks engaged, feet non-slip, rated for user weight + tools
- [ ] Drop cloths fully covering floors and secured at edges
- [ ] SDS sheets reviewed for all products in use and accessible on site
- [ ] Fire extinguisher accessible if solvent-based products are used

---

## 6. Tools & Equipment

### 6.1 Required Tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| 9" roller frame with extension pole | Rolling paint on wall field |
| Roller covers — 3/8" nap (smooth) or 1/2" nap (textured) | Texture-matched coverage |
| 2.5" angled sash brush | Cutting in at ceiling, corners, and trim edges |
| 2" angled brush | Tight corners and detail areas |
| Paint tray with liner | Holding and loading roller |
| 5-in-1 painter's tool | Opening cans, cleaning roller, scraping |
| Putty knife (4") | Applying spackling compound |
| Sanding block | Feathering repairs flush |
| Sandpaper — 120-grit and 220-grit | Surface prep and feathering |
| Painter's tape — 1.5" and 2" | Masking trim, outlets, and adjacent surfaces |
| Drop cloths | Floor and furniture protection |
| Step ladder (6-ft) | Access to upper walls and ceiling edge |
| Stir sticks | Mixing paint and primer |
| Drill with mixing paddle (optional) | Thorough mixing of large volumes |
| Damp rags and clean cloths | Surface wipe-down and drip cleanup |

### 6.2 Required Materials

| Material | Specification |
|---|---|
| Interior latex primer | PVA primer for new drywall; shellac or stain-blocking primer for water stains or smoke; standard latex primer for repaint |
| Interior latex paint — finish coat | Customer-selected color and sheen (eggshell or satin recommended for walls); low-VOC preferred |
| Lightweight spackling compound | For holes up t

<< 13694 Characters hidden >>

ity below 85% during application and drying. Extreme cold, heat, or humidity affects dry time and adhesion.

#### Step 7: Remove Tape & Final Inspection

1. Remove painter's tape **before the paint fully cures** — ideally when paint is dry to the touch but not hard (approximately 1–2 hrs after final coat).
2. Pull tape back at a 45° angle toward the painted surface (not away from it) to prevent lifting paint.
3. If the tape pulls paint, score the tape edge lightly with a utility knife before pulling.
4. Inspect the entire room in raking light: look for holidays, sags, lap marks, and tape bleed.
5. Touch up as needed with a small brush, feathering edges into the surrounding surface.

---

| Step 2: Cutting In Technique | 🔍 *Video to be sourced* | — | — |
| Step 3: Rolling Technique & Wet Edge | 🔍 *Video to be sourced* | — | — |
| Steps 5–6: Second Coat & Finishing | 🔍 *Video to be sourced* | — | — |
| Step 7: Tape Removal | 🔍 *Video to be sourced* | — | — |

> 📹 **Curation notes:** Prefer content from paint manufacturer channels (Sherwin-Williams, Benjamin Moore) and licensed trade professionals. Verify links at each SOP revision. Maximum 5 videos per phase.

---

## 12. Testing & Commissioning

### Step 1: Visual Inspection

- [ ] No visible brush marks, sags, drips, or roller stipple from 5 ft viewing distance
- [ ] Uniform sheen across entire wall surface — no flat or shiny spots
- [ ] Full coverage — no holidays (missed spots) or thin areas showing substrate or previous color
- [ ] Clean paint lines at ceiling, trim, and adjacent walls — no tape bleed
- [ ] All patches invisible under the final coat

### Step 2: Color & Sheen Verification

- [ ] Color matches customer-approved sample in the room's natural and artificial lighting
- [ ] Sheen level consistent with specification (eggshell, satin, or flat)
- [ ] No color variation between sections (batch mismatch or inconsistent mixing)

### Step 3: Final Verification

- [ ] All painter's tape removed without pulling existing paint
- [ ] Outlet covers and switch plates reinstalled
- [ ] Drop cloths removed; floor free of paint drips
- [ ] No paint on trim, windows, ceiling, or flooring (or touch-up of overspray complete)

---

## 13. Cleanup & Closeout

See [REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md](REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 7: Standard Cleanup, Documentation & Customer Handoff.

**Additional steps for this SOP:**

- Clean rollers: use a roller spinner tool over a bucket; rinse until water runs clear; dispose of if badly worn.
- Reinstall outlet covers, switch plates, and light fixture trim rings before leaving the space.

---

## 14. Troubleshooting

| Issue | Probable Cause | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| Paint peeling after drying | Poor surface prep; contamination; insufficient primer | Strip affected section, clean thoroughly, prime correctly, repaint |
| Lap marks / roller lines visible | Rolling into dry sections; wrong nap size; paint drying too fast | Maintain wet edge; work smaller sections; add conditioner (e.g., Floetrol) in hot/dry conditions |
| Drips and sags | Over-applied paint; coat applied too thick | Allow to dry fully; sand smooth; apply thinner coat |
| Blotchy or uneven color | Insufficient primer on porous patches; skipped prime coat | Apply PVA primer to all patched areas; repaint |
| Sheen variation between sections | Different paint batches; inconsistent technique | Box all cans before starting; apply consistent pressure and roller speed |
| Paint bleed under tape | Tape not pressed firmly; surface dirty under tape | Score tape edge carefully; remove tape; touch up with small brush |
| Brush marks at cut-in visible | Paint drying in brush between cut-in and rolling | Immediately roll into wet cut-in; use conditioner in brush paint to extend open time |
| Stains bleeding through topcoat | Inadequate stain-blocking primer | Apply shellac-based primer over stained areas; repaint |

---

## 15. Quality Standards

- No visible brush marks, roller lines, sags, or drips at normal viewing distance (5 ft)
- Uniform sheen across the entire wall surface — no inconsistencies
- Clean paint lines at ceiling, trim, and adjacent walls — gaps ≤ 1/16"
- Full opacity — no substrate, previous color, or patch visible
- Color confirmed to match customer-approved sample in room lighting
- All repairs fully covered and invisible under final coat
- No paint on trim, glass, flooring, or ceiling (or thoroughly cleaned)

---

## 16. Revision History

| Rev | Date | Author | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1.0 | 2026-03-08 | — | Initial release |
| 1.1 | 2026-03-08 | — | Shared sections (§3–§5.2, §13) extracted to REF-PNT-001; SOP-specific content retained inline |

---

*This SOP is a living document. Report any errors, omissions, or improvement suggestions to the Operations Manager.*
\n\n=== REF PNT Codes Standards ===
# Reference Document — Painting Trade: Codes & Standards

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Document ID** | REF-PNT-001 |
| **Applies To** | All PNT-SOP-### procedures |
| **Revision** | 1.1 |
| **Effective Date** | 2026-03-08 |

---

## 1. Regulatory References

- **EPA RRP Rule (40 CFR Part 745)** — Lead-safe work practices required for pre-1978 structures; applies to all sanding, scraping, and surface preparation on painted surfaces
- **OSHA 29 CFR 1926.62** — Lead exposure limits and controls in construction
- **OSHA 29 CFR 1910.94** — Ventilation requirements for spray and brush application of coatings

## 2. Codes & Standards

- **Local jurisdiction VOC limits** — Verify maximum allowable g/L for architectural coatings before product selection; limits vary by air quality district
- **PDCA Standards (Painting and Decorating Contractors of America)** — Industry workmanship standards for surface preparation and finish quality

## 3. Documentation Requirements

- **Paint manufacturer application instructions** — Required reading before applying any product; conditions, spread rate, recoat time, and compatibility vary by product line
- **SDS sheets (Safety Data Sheets)** — Must be on-site and reviewed before use for all paints, primers, solvents, and caulks

---

## 4. Personnel & Qualifications

Applies to all painting SOPs unless the individual SOP specifies otherwise.

| Role | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Lead Painter | Journeyman painter or equivalent field experience; EPA RRP Certified if structure built before 1978 |
| Painter's Helper | Under direct supervision of Lead Painter; no independent work on pre-1978 surfaces without Lead Painter present |

---

## 5. Standard PPE

Required for all painting procedures unless the individual SOP notes exceptions or additions.

- Safety glasses or goggles — splash protection when opening cans, pouring, or rolling; required when working overhead
- Nitrile gloves — chemical resistance to paint, primer, caulk, and solvents
- N95 respirator or P100 half-mask — required when sanding any surface; VOC-rated respirator required if using solvent-based products
- Non-slip closed-toe footwear
- Protective coveralls (recommended to prevent clothing contamination)
- Knee pads — required for baseboard-level and low-surface work

---

## 6. Common Hazard Awareness

The following hazards apply across all painting procedures. Individual SOPs list additional task-specific hazards.

| Hazard | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Lead paint disturbance (pre-1978 structures) | Test surfaces before any sanding or scraping; follow EPA RRP protocol; never dry-sand lead paint; use HEPA vacuum on all sanding dust |
| VOC inhalation | Open windows and exterior doors; use box fans exhausting to exterior; use low-VOC or zero-VOC products where possible |
| Eye splash from paint | Wear safety glasses; do not work overhead without eye protection |
| Ladder / step stool fall | Use ladder rated for combined weight of user and materials; maintain 3-point contact; never overreach |
| Skin and respiratory irritation | Wear nitrile gloves and respirator when sanding or using solvent-based products; wash skin immediately on contact |

---

## 7. Standard Cleanup, Documentation & Customer Handoff

### Cleanup

1. Remove all painter's tape — pull slowly at a 45° angle toward the painted surface.
2. Remove drop cloths; fold paint-side in; clean or dispose of properly.
3. Inspect floor for paint drips — clean wet paint immediately with a damp cloth; dried latex can be gently scraped with a plastic scraper.
4. Clean brushes: rinse latex paint brushes under warm water, working paint out from heel to tip; hang or lay flat to dry.
5. Reseal all paint cans tightly; label with date opened, color, sheen, and room.

### Documentation

1. Record paint color name, code, formula, sheen, and brand on the work order.
2. Photograph completed work — one photo per wall or surface area, angled in raking light to reveal finish quality.
3. Log quantity of paint used per room or area.
4. Note any conditions flagged for follow-up (e.g., monitored stain areas, cracked joints, active settlement).

### Customer Handoff

- Walk customer through the completed space; identify any areas that may benefit from a future touch-up coat.
- Provide leftover paint in a clearly labeled container for customer touch-ups.
- Advise: ventilate the space for 24–48 hrs after completion.
- Advise: allow full cure time before cleaning or placing items against painted surfaces (typically 24–48 hrs touch-safe; 7 days for full hardness).
- Obtain customer sign-off on the work order.

---

## Revision History

| Rev | Date | Author | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1.0 | 2026-03-08 | — | Initial release; extracted from PNT-SOP-001 and PNT-SOP-004 |
| 1.1 | 2026-03-08 | — | Added §4 Personnel, §5 PPE, §6 Common Hazards, §7 Standard Cleanup |

=== PNT Trim SOP (first 80 lines) ===
# Standard Operating Procedure
## Painting — Trim & Baseboard Painting

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **SOP Number** | PNT-SOP-004 |
| **Trade** | Painting |
| **Task** | Interior trim and baseboard painting — surface prep, prime, and 2-coat brush finish |
| **Revision** | 1.0 |
| **Effective Date** | 2026-03-08 |
| **Approved By** | DRAFT |
| **Estimated Duration** | 2–4 hrs per room (varies by linear footage and condition) |

---

## 1. Purpose

This SOP defines the standard procedure for preparing, priming, and painting interior trim and baseboard surfaces including door casings, window casings, baseboards, and crown molding. It ensures a clean, durable, and professional finish consistent with industry quality standards.

---

## 2. Scope

This SOP covers:
- Interior baseboards, door casings, window casings, and crown molding
- Priming bare wood, MDF, or previously painted trim in poor condition
- Application of 2-coat semi-gloss or gloss brush finish on trim surfaces

This SOP does **not** cover:
- Interior wall painting (see PNT-SOP-001: Interior Wall Painting)
- Exterior trim or fascia painting (see PNT-SOP-002: Exterior Siding Painting)
- Cabinet face or door painting (see PNT-SOP-003: Cabinet Repainting)
- Full trim removal and reinstallation (see CRP-SOP-001: Interior Door Replacement)
- Wood staining or clear coat finish — this SOP covers opaque paint only

---

## 3. References & Codes

See [REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md](REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md) — Painting Trade: Codes & Standards Reference.

> ⚠️ **Code Check:** Trim and windows in pre-1978 homes are among the highest-risk surfaces for lead paint. Test before sanding. Follow EPA RRP (40 CFR Part 745) protocol strictly.

---

## 4. Personnel & Qualifications

See [REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md](REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 4: Personnel & Qualifications.

**SOP-specific note:** Painter's Helper may prep and mask but may not sand independently on pre-1978 surfaces.

---

## 5. Safety Requirements

### 5.1 Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)

See [REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md](REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 5: Standard PPE.

**SOP-specific note:** Knee pads are essential (not optional) for all baseboard work.

### 5.2 Hazard Awareness

See [REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md](REF_PNT_Codes_Standards.md) — Section 6: Common Hazard Awareness.

**Additional hazards for this SOP:**

| Hazard | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Prolonged kneeling (baseboard work) | Use knee pads; take breaks; alternate kneeling and squatting |

### 5.3 Pre-Work Safety Checklist

- [ ] Lead paint test completed on any trim surface to be sanded in a pre-1978 structure
- [ ] Ventilation confirmed: windows open or fan exhausting to exterior
- [ ] Knee pads available for baseboard work
- [ ] Ladder inspected — rated for user weight, non-slip feet, all locks engaged
- [ ] SDS sheets reviewed for all products and accessible on site
- [ ] Drop cloths placed under all work areas to catch drips

# Standard Operating Procedure
## Plumbing — Sink Replacement

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **SOP Number** | PLB-SOP-001 |
| **Trade** | Plumbing |
| **Task** | Remove & Replace Sink (Kitchen / Bathroom / Utility) |
| **Revision** | 1.0 |
| **Effective Date** | 2026-02-26 |
| **Approved By** | _________________________ |
| **Estimated Duration** | 2 – 4 hours (varies by scope) |

---

## 1. Purpose

This procedure defines the safe, code-compliant steps for removing an existing sink and installing a replacement sink, including all associated supply lines, drain connections, and fixtures. It applies to kitchen, bathroom, and utility sinks in residential and light-commercial settings.

---

## 2. Scope

This SOP covers:
- Disconnection and removal of the existing sink, faucet, and drain assembly
- Preparation of the countertop or mounting surface
- Installation of the new sink, faucet, supply lines, and drain assembly
- Leak testing and final inspection

This SOP does **not** cover:
- Countertop fabrication or modification beyond standard sink cutout adjustments
- Relocation of supply or drain rough-in (see SOP PLB-SOP-010)
- Garbage disposal installation (see SOP PLB-SOP-005)
- Dishwasher connection (see SOP PLB-SOP-006)

---

## 3. References & Codes

- International Plumbing Code (IPC) — current edition
- Uniform Plumbing Code (UPC) — current edition (where applicable)
- Local jurisdiction amendments
- Manufacturer installation instructions for specific sink and faucet models
- OSHA 29 CFR 1926 — Construction Industry Standards

---

## 4. Personnel & Qualifications

| Role | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Lead Plumber | Licensed journeyman plumber or equivalent |
| Apprentice (if assisting) | Enrolled apprentice under direct supervision |
| Inspector (if required) | Local AHJ plumbing inspector for final sign-off |

---

## 5. Safety Requirements

### 5.1 Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
- Safety glasses / goggles
- Work gloves (cut-resistant recommended)
- Knee pads (for under-cabinet work)
- Steel-toe boots
- Dust mask (if cutting or grinding)

### 5.2 Hazard Awareness
| Hazard | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Water damage | Shut off supply valves; have towels/bucket ready |
| Sharp edges | Wear cut-resistant gloves; deburr all cut pipe |
| Mold / mildew exposure | Wear N95 mask if mold is present; report to supervisor |
| Electrical proximity | Verify no live wiring near work area; use GFCI-protected circuits |
| Back strain | Use proper lifting technique; get assistance for heavy sinks (cast iron, stone) |
| Lead solder (older homes) | Test if pre-1986 plumbing; follow lead-safe work practices if positive |

### 5.3 Pre-Work Safety Checklist
- [ ] Water supply shut off and verified (both hot and cold)
- [ ] Work area cleared and protected (drop cloths, floor protection)
- [ ] Electrical circuits near sink area identified
\n=== ELC SOP (first 80 lines) ===
# Standard Operating Procedure
## Electrical — Outlet Replacement

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **SOP Number** | ELC-SOP-001 |
| **Trade** | Electrical |
| **Task** | Remove & Replace Electrical Outlet (Receptacle) — 120V/240V Residential & Light-Commercial |
| **Revision** | 1.0 |
| **Effective Date** | 2026-02-28 |
| **Approved By** | _________________________ |
| **Estimated Duration** | 30 min – 2 hours (varies by scope and circuit complexity) |

---

## 1. Purpose

This procedure defines the safe, code-compliant steps for removing an existing electrical outlet (receptacle) and installing a replacement. It covers standard duplex receptacles, GFCI receptacles, USB-equipped receptacles, and 240V receptacles in residential and light-commercial settings.

---

## 2. Scope

This SOP covers:
- De-energizing the circuit and verifying zero-energy state
- Removal of the existing receptacle, cover plate, and wiring connections
- Installation of the replacement receptacle with proper wiring terminations
- Testing for correct wiring, grounding, and GFCI protection
- Cover plate installation and final commissioning

This SOP does **not** cover:
- New circuit installation or home run wiring (see ELC-SOP-005: New Branch Circuit Installation)
- Electrical panel upgrades or breaker replacement (see ELC-SOP-010: Panel & Breaker Service)
- Whole-room rewiring or knob-and-tube replacement (see ELC-SOP-015: Legacy Wiring Remediation)
- Dedicated 240V circuit installation for appliances (see ELC-SOP-020: Dedicated Appliance Circuit)
- Smart outlet / home automation integration (see ELC-SOP-025: Smart Device Installation)
- Outdoor / wet-location receptacle installation requiring new weatherproof boxes (see ELC-SOP-030: Exterior Receptacle Installation)

---

## 3. References & Codes

- National Electrical Code (NEC / NFPA 70) — current edition
- Local jurisdiction amendments and permit requirements
- Manufacturer installation instructions for specific receptacle models
- OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart K — Electrical (Construction)
- OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Subpart S — Electrical (General Industry)
- NFPA 70E — Standard for Electrical Safety in the Workplace

---

## 4. Personnel & Qualifications

| Role | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Lead Electrician | Licensed journeyman electrician or equivalent per jurisdiction |
| Apprentice (if assisting) | Enrolled electrical apprentice under direct supervision |
| Inspector (if required) | Local AHJ electrical inspector for final sign-off |

---

## 5. Safety Requirements

### 5.1 Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
- Safety glasses / goggles
- Insulated electrical gloves (rated for voltage class — minimum Class 00 for 120V)
- Leather protector gloves over insulated gloves
- Non-conductive / dielectric footwear
- Arc-rated clothing (if panel work required — minimum 8 cal/cm²)
- Hearing protection (if using power tools)

### 5.2 Hazard Awareness
| Hazard | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Electrical shock (120V/240V) | LOTO procedure; verify zero-energy with rated tester; never trust a single test |
| Arc flash | Wear arc-rated PPE; maintain safe approach boundaries per NFPA 70E |
| Fire risk | Verify wire gauge matches breaker rating; ensure tight connections; no backstab on 20A circuits |
| Damaged / deteriorated wiring | Inspect all visible wiring; report and remediate before proceeding |
| Asbestos (older homes) | If suspected in wall material, stop work and report; do not disturb |
| Aluminum wiring (pre-1976 homes) | Identify by color (silver vs. copper); use CO/ALR rated devices only; apply anti-oxidant compound |
